[154:15]said, that's a possibility on Monday. They attack Beirut knowing Iran must retaliate

[154:21]so that they could get things started again. And I don't know if I said this on Monday

[161:50]And as I have always said in this process,

[161:53]time is the critical variable.

[161:56]If you remove that from the equation,

[161:58]we could do this forever.

[162:01]We could trade messages

[162:02]and we could talk about a framework for negotiations

[162:06]and we could talk about short-term ceasefires

[162:08]and long-term pieces,

[162:10]but the function here is time.

[162:14]And the reason the time is so critical is because the longer the ceasefire goes on, the more that Iran rebuilds and repairs its missile stockpile and its missile production capability, and the same with drones and the same with their nuclear hedge.

Topic 7: Part 4 (running out of time & a complete loss)

[162:31]The more time they are not being bombed actively by the United States, the more time they have
